## About Branch
Branch focuses on transforming digital interactions between brands and users. The company offers solutions to deliver experiences, return on investment, wasted spend, and siloed attribution by integrating their technology into various marketing channels such as applications, web, email, social media, search, and paid advertisements. It serves the mobile ecosystem, with a customer base that includes a range of applications. It was formerly known as Pawprint Labs. It was founded in 2014 and is based in Palo Alto, California.

## Branch's Products & Differentiators
Universal Ads
Complicated, cross-platform experiences are the norm and your mobile measurement partner (MMP) needs to keep up. Complex conversions that legacy systems miss or, worse, put into the wrong bucket, now show up where they belong. Optimize with granular data including down-funnel events combined across platforms, conversion rates compared by ad network, and long-term trends broken down by platform, ad set name, channel, and more. Retarget app users who see a web ad and then purchase in the app, attribute revenue to the web ad that drove the install, and measure cumulative revenue from users across both web and app. Increase conversions by taking new users straight to specific ad content after install, and run engagement campaigns to your existing users with built-in support for Universal Links and Android App Links.
[![Start free trial](https://no-cache.hubspot.com/cta/default/763793/55926c7d-e7c1-4c17-8ceb-37665ca53a2f.png)](https://cta-redirect.hubspot.com/cta/redirect/763793/55926c7d-e7c1-4c17-8ceb-37665ca53a2f)

## Latest Branch News
[Mobile Marketing Technology: Platforms, Push Notifications and the App Economy](https://techbullion.com/mobile-marketing-technology-platforms-push-notifications-and-the-app-economy/)
Mar 9, 2026
in many emerging markets mobile is the primary, often only, means of accessing digital services. The mobile marketing technology sector, valued at $6.8 billion in 2025, encompasses the platforms, infrastructure and analytics tools that enable brands to reach, engage and convert audiences on smartphones and tablets. Understanding this ecosystem is essential for any organisation serious about digital marketing effectiveness. The Mobile Marketing Technology Landscape Mobile marketing technology spans a broad spectrum from channel-specific communication tools to sophisticated analytics and attribution platforms. The ecosystem can be broadly divided into five categories: mobile advertising technology, which enables targeted paid acquisition on mobile devices; messaging platforms covering push notifications, in-app messaging and SMS; mobile analytics and attribution, which measure engagement and conversion across mobile touchpoints; app store optimisation tools; and mobile-specific personalisation and engagement platforms. The complexity of mobile marketing is compounded by platform fragmentation. iOS and Android represent fundamentally different technical environments with distinct development requirements, app store policies and advertising ecosystems. Each platform has also implemented privacy controls that significantly affect the data available to marketers. Apple's App Tracking Transparency framework, introduced in 2021, required apps to explicitly request permission to track users across apps and websites for advertising purposes. The result was a dramatic reduction in available mobile advertising identifiers, forcing significant adaptation across the entire mobile marketing technology ecosystem. Push Notification Platforms Push notifications remain one of the highest-engagement channels available to mobile marketers. When delivered with appropriate timing and relevance, push notifications achieve open rates far exceeding email, with direct links into app experiences that reduce friction for conversion. The technology enabling sophisticated push notification programmes has advanced considerably beyond simple broadcast messages to support rich media, interactive notifications, personalised content and intelligent delivery optimisation. Platforms including Braze, Airship and CleverTap provide enterprise-grade push notification infrastructure with personalisation capabilities, A/B testing, delivery time optimisation and detaile

tavily.answer:
The official website for Branch (company) is https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Branch_(company). Branch is a software company focused on deep linking and attribution.
Branch is a company providing financial services, office furniture, mobile gaming, and software solutions. It was founded in 2015 and has multiple branches in different industries. The company is headquartered in Mountain View, California.
Branch is a mobile attribution platform that specializes in deep linking and analytics. It helps businesses track user journeys and optimize marketing ROI. Founded in 2014, it serves over 100,000 customers globally.
Wellfound is a hiring platform for startups, offering job listings and tools to connect companies with tech talent. It features an AI-driven recruiter and has a large pool of startup-focused candidates. The platform provides both free and paid options for companies to post jobs.
DealRoom is a software platform for mergers and acquisitions, founded in 2012, and raised $7 million in funding in 2026. It provides tools for deal sourcing, diligence, and integration. The company is based in Boston, Massachusetts.
